Missing HttpOnly: Set the HttpOnly flag on sensitive cookies to prevent access from client-side scripts and reduce XSS risk.
Not Secure: Set the Secure flag on cookies to ensure they are only sent over HTTPS channels.
Tracking: Review tracking cookies and implement explicit consent controls to align with privacy and compliance requirements.
Analytics: Limit analytics cookie exposure and document their use for privacy reviews and stakeholder reporting.
Expired Cookie: Remove expired or stale cookies from storage to reduce persistence risk and improve session hygiene.
Third-party: Restrict third-party cookie usage where possible and isolate cross-site tracking to minimize privacy risk.
Large Value: Avoid oversized cookie values; keep payloads small to reduce network overhead and fingerprinting surface.
Session: Review session cookies for appropriate lifetime and protect them with Secure and HttpOnly flags.
SameSite=None without Secure: SameSite=None cookies must be Secure, or they create a high cross-site data exposure risk.
Public: Avoid storing sensitive data in publicly accessible cookies; use secure, server-side session controls instead.
